title: Fans Get Ready for the World Cup

source.title: How fans are feeling ahead of the World Cup

segments.title: A New Look
segments.audioText: Seattle's Space Needle sported a new look on Saturday, painted like a soccer ball.
segments.transcript: Seattle's Space Needle sported a new look on Saturday, painted like a soccer ball.
segments.translationZh: 西雅圖太空針塔星期六換咗新造型，被髹到好似一個足球咁。

points.label: sported a new look
points.explanationZh: sport a new look 係新聞同生活都會用嘅講法，意思係『換咗新造型』。

points.label: painted like a soccer ball
points.explanationZh: painted like... 係『被髹到似...』；聽到 like 後面留意比喻對象。

points.label: on Saturday
points.explanationZh: 時間放句尾，聽新聞時可以當作補充背景。

segments.title: Host Cities
segments.audioText: The city is one of 16 across North America set to host men's World Cup games.
segments.transcript: The city is one of 16 across North America set to host men's World Cup games.
segments.translationZh: 呢個城市係北美 16 個將會主辦男子世界盃賽事嘅城市之一。

points.label: one of 16
points.explanationZh: one of 加數字或複數名詞，表示『其中之一』，新聞講名單、城市、公司好常見。

points.label: across North America
points.explanationZh: across 係橫跨、遍及；North America 指北美。

points.label: set to host
points.explanationZh: set to do something 即將會、已安排好做某事；host 係主辦。

segments.title: Fans Getting Ready
segments.audioText: Nicole Valdes reports on how die-hard fans are getting ready.
segments.transcript: Nicole Valdes reports on how die-hard fans are getting ready.
segments.translationZh: Nicole Valdes 報道忠實球迷點樣準備迎接賽事。

points.label: reports on
points.explanationZh: report on something 即報道某個題目；on 後面係主題。

points.label: die-hard fans
points.explanationZh: die-hard fans 指非常忠實、好投入嘅粉絲，語氣自然。

points.label: are getting ready
points.explanationZh: get ready 係準備；are getting ready 表示而家正準備緊。

sentences: Seattle's Space Needle sported a new look on Saturday, painted like a soccer ball.
The city is one of 16 across North America set to host men's World Cup games.
Nicole Valdes reports on how die-hard fans are getting ready.

vocabulary.meaningZh: 展示；穿戴；以某種造型出現
vocabulary.example: The tower sported a new look for the event.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢座塔為咗活動換咗新造型。

vocabulary.example: The building was painted like a soccer ball.
vocabulary.translationZh: 嗰座建築被髹到好似足球咁。

vocabulary.example: The city will host several World Cup games.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢個城市會主辦幾場世界盃賽事。

vocabulary.example: Fans are excited ahead of the tournament.
vocabulary.translationZh: 比賽即將到來，球迷都好興奮。

vocabulary.example: Die-hard fans waited outside the stadium.
vocabulary.translationZh: 死忠球迷喺球場外面等。

vocabulary.example: The fans are getting ready for the big game.
vocabulary.translationZh: 球迷正準備迎接呢場大賽。

phrases.meaningZh: 換上新造型；以新面貌出現
phrases.example: The Space Needle sported a new look.
Seattle is one of 16 host cities.
The city is set to host World Cup games.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 跟讀第一句，留意 sported a new look 呢個自然講法。
重播 00:12，先寫低 one of 16，再補 across North America。
用 set to host 練替換：The city is set to host a big concert.
用兩句英文複述：Seattle is getting ready for the World Cup. Fans are excited for the games.

notes: 今日重點係聽體育同城市準備新聞入面嘅短句，句子短，適合做盲聽同跟讀。
先用時間碼聽三句，不要急住睇稿；第二次先對照 transcript。
one of 16、set to host、getting ready 都係新聞同日常都常用嘅 chunk。
dailyLifePractice 會配合活動同出行主題，練約人、問路同解釋小延誤。

dailyLifePractice.title: Meeting Before a Game
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你同朋友約好睇比賽，要自然確認集合時間同地點。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Are we still meeting at six? I can meet you by the main entrance. Text me when you get close.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Are we still meeting at six?

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Yes, that still works for me.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I can meet you by the main entrance.
lines.zh: 我可以喺正門附近等你。

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Perfect. I'll text you when I get close.
lines.zh: 好。我差唔多到就短訊你。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Are we still...? 係好自然嘅確認問法，唔會太正式。by the main entrance 指喺正門附近，比 inside the entrance 更有彈性。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 six 換成 seven-thirty、noon 或 after work。
drills.example: Are we still meeting after work?

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 Text me when you get close 三次。
drills.example: Text me when you get close.

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking for Directions
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你去大型活動，搵唔到入口，要簡短問職員。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Excuse me, is this the line for Gate B? I'm trying to find the entrance for section 112. Thanks, I appreciate it.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Excuse me, is this the line for Gate B?
lines.zh: 唔好意思，呢條係去 B 閘嘅隊嗎？

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: Gate B is around the corner.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Got it. I'm trying to find the entrance for section 112.
lines.zh: 明白。我想搵 112 區嘅入口。

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: You're close. Just keep going straight.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Is this the line for...? 喺美國排隊場景好常用。I appreciate it 比單講 thanks 稍為更暖、更有禮貌。

drills.label: Role-play
drills.prompt: 用 Is this the line for...? 問另一個地點。
drills.example: Is this the line for the shuttle?

drills.label: Short response
drills.prompt: 聽到方向後，用 Got it 加一句你要去邊。
drills.example: Got it. I'm trying to find section 205.

dailyLifePractice.title: Explaining a Small Delay
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你去活動路上遇到人多或交通慢，要輕鬆通知朋友會遲少少。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I'm running a little behind. The train is packed, but I'm almost there. Save me a spot if you can.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: The train is packed, but I'm almost there.
lines.zh: 架車好迫，不過我差唔多到。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Save me a spot if you can.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: I'm running a little behind 比 I'm late 柔和，適合預先通知。packed 係口語，講車廂、餐廳、場館好多人都可以用。

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 I'm running a little behind 開頭，換一個原因。
drills.example: I'm running a little behind. Traffic is heavier than I expected.

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 Save me... if you can 講一個簡短請求。
drills.example: Save me a seat if you can.
